title: Market Sizing Model for Document Automation
author: Research Team
abstract: This report evaluates the addressable market for agent-driven document production platforms. We model adoption curves under three scenarios and estimate revenue potential for vertical SaaS integrators. Key drivers include MCP adoption, Typst accessibility defaults, and enterprise demand for auditable PDF workflows.

sections.title: Methodology
sections.body_md:
We surveyed **120 automation teams** and modeled TAM/SAM/SOM using:

- Bottom-up seat counts
- Top-down enterprise spend benchmarks

sections.title: Competitive Landscape
sections.body: Incumbent document compilers optimize for human authors. Agent-native pipelines remain underserved, creating whitespace for schema-first products.

findings.title: MCP document tools are early but accelerating
findings.summary: 62% of surveyed teams plan a PDF deliverable step within 12 months.

findings.title: Schema-first templates reduce agent failure rates
findings.summary: Structured JSON workflows cut compile-retry loops by an estimated 40% in pilot programs.
